title: decalepis arayalpathra
intro: Decalepis arayalpathra, also known as the 'Indian gentian', is an endangered, succulent plant native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Decalepis arayalpathra include reducing fever, treating digestive disorders, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusion, decoction, and powder, which can be used to create teas, tonics, and supplements.
